title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
New Tax Regime Deductions and Exemptions
As an individual taxpayer, it is crucial that you get a good understanding of what plans and deductions are best suited. With the introduction of the new tax regime, you might find it daunting to get into the details of every aspect. However, a quick overall study can help you get a good understanding of the new aspects for better planning of your finances. To begin with, the first step is to understand the bracket you belong to. These brackets are carefully designed for different types of employees, including salaried and self-employed.
With each income slab, comes different kinds of tax deductions that you have to understand. The work might sound daunting but it is interesting and easier than you have thought. The simple way is to understand the fundamentals and then claim deductions available under the New Tax Regime. The article will help you understand two major aspects- Exemption and Deductions under New Tax Regime.
New tax regime - Deductions and Exemptions
To begin with, deductions under the New Tax Regime require you not to put your hope into the earlier provisions. Here are some of the provisions that you can study and claim available decuctions1.Below is the list of deductions allowed and not allowed under New Tax Regime:
The government in Budget 2023 introduced 5 key changes to encourage taxpayers to adopt the new regime. They are:
Higher Tax Rebate Limit: Full tax rebate on an income up to ₹7 lakhs has been introduced. Whereas, this threshold is ₹5 lakhs under the old tax regime. This means that taxpayers with an income of up to ₹7 lakhs will not have to pay any tax at all under the new tax regime!
Streamlined Tax Slabs: The tax exemption limit has been increased to ₹3 lakhs
Standard Deduction and Family Pension Deduction:
Salary income: The standard deduction of ₹50,000, which was only available under the old regime, has now been extended to the new tax regime as well. This, along with the rebate, makes ₹7.5 lakhs as your tax-free income under the new regime.
Family pension: Those receiving family pension can claim a deduction of: ₹15,000 or 1/3rd of pension, whichever is lower.
Reduced Surcharge for High Net Worth Individuals: The surcharge rate on income over ₹5 crores has been reduced from 37% to 25%. This move will bring down their effective tax rate 42.74% to 39%.
Default Regime: Starting from FY 2023-24, the new income tax regime will be set as the default option.
Conclusion
A salaried taxpayer can choose the new tax regime at the beginning of FY 2023-24 and intimate their employer. The employee cannot change their choice anytime during the financial year. However, they can change their tax regime when filing the income tax return for the financial year 2023-24.
The due date for tax filing for the FY 2022-23 (AY 2023-24) is 31st July 2023.
In case an employee does not choose the new tax regime at the beginning of the financial year, the employer will deduct tax (TDS) under the existing tax regime. A salaried taxpayer can choose the new tax regime in one year and choose theold tax regime in another year.
The above-mentioned provisions cover all major aspects of your finance. This helps you decide and rethink your plans more efficiently. Deductions under the new tax regime have been planned carefully to ensure balanced and egalitarian finance management. The division does not favour any group, and all the deductions and exemptions depend from individual to individual and the plans they choose. You are only required to understand your tax slab and plan to get the most out of the new tax regime.
FAQs
What are the new tax rates for Income Tax?
The new tax rates for income tax are as follows for FY 2023-244-
Can I choose between the old and the new tax regime?
Yes, you can. If you are a salaried employee then you have to choose it at the beginning of FY 2023-24 and inform your employer. If you are a non-salaried employee then you have to choose while filing a tax return.You cannot change it between a financial year. If you fail to make a choice, then for the said financial year, you will be subjected to the New tax regime. From FY 2023-24, by default new tax regime will be applicable and one has to choose old tax regime at the time of filing return of income.
How to choose the new tax regime?
There isn’t any hard and fast rule. The simplest way forward is to carefully study the exemption and deductions you will enjoy in your existing schemes and plans. If needed, it is always recommended to take financial advice from a professional. Moreover, as per the Union Budget 2023, the new tax regime will be made default5 unless you choose otherwise. So, if you file your taxes, they will be automatically filed under the new tax regime unless you opt for the old regime.
What are the exemptions and deductions not allowed under new tax regime that were allowed in the old tax regime?
Check out below some of the deductions which are not allowed:
Leave travel concession (LTC) is applicable to all salaried employees.
House Rent Allowance (HRA) is applicable to all salaried employees.
Deduction for entertainment along with professional tax.
Deductions under Chapter VIA (other than the provisions of sub-section (2) of section 80CCD)
For a detailed list, it is recommended that you refer Section 115BAC of Income Tax Act, 1961.
